issues.summary: PosGroup constants live in `query` but are imported by `config` and `tagging`, violating dependency direction
evidence: PosGroup.java is in package `query`; GrammarConfigLoader.java imports `pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.PosGroup`; SimpleTagger.java imports the same
`config` and `tagging` are infrastructure/config packages that should not depend on the `query` layer
issues.suggestion: Move PosGroup to a shared `model` or `common` package (e.g., `pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model.PosGroup`) so it sits below both `query` and `config` in the dependency graph.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer accesses public fields of SemanticFieldExplorer inner result classes, breaching the query/api boundary
evidence: WordSketchApiServer line 506: `result.seedAdjectives`, line 519: `dn.noun`, `dn.sharedCount`, `dn.similarityScore`, `dn.avgLogDice`
SemanticFieldExplorer inner classes DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, Edge expose public fields rather than accessor methods
The API layer bypasses encapsulation and couples directly to internal data-holder layout
issues.suggestion: Add accessor methods to DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ExplorationResult, and Edge; remove public fields. WordSketchApiServer should use only those getters.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer is a 994-line god class mixing HTTP routing with all business/domain logic for 10+ endpoints
evidence: start() method alone is ~260 lines registering all routes as inline lambdas
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti is 125+ lines of domain logic (intersection of collocate sets, edge building) inside the API handler
handleSemanticField, handleConcordanceExamples, handleFullSketch, handleFullDependencySketch, handleRelationQuery each also carry full result-assembly logic
issues.suggestion: Extract route registration into a `registerRoutes()` helper per logical group. Move multi-seed intersection logic into SemanticFieldExplorer as an `exploreMulti()` method. Delegate response serialization to a dedicated mapper/serializer class.

issues.summary: Two methods appear implemented but are stubs that always return placeholder values
evidence: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern() lines 164-170: computes a label position but unconditionally returns `"unknown"` regardless
BlackLabQueryExecutor.getCorpusSize() returns hardcoded `0L` with no comment indicating it is intentionally unimplemented
issues.suggestion: Either implement extractCollocateFromPattern properly (it has all required logic already available in sibling methods) or remove it and eliminate dead call sites. Mark getCorpusSize() with a TODO comment or throw UnsupportedOperationException if not yet needed.

issues.summary: Multi-seed exploration logic is implemented inline in the API handler instead of inside SemanticFieldExplorer
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (lines 567-691) builds seedToCollocates map, computes commonCollocates intersection, and builds edge list entirely in the API handler
SemanticFieldExplorer already has an exploreByPattern() method for single-seed; no multi-seed equivalent exists in the domain class
issues.suggestion: Add `SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMulti(Set<String> seeds, ...)` returning a result object. Move the intersection and edge logic there. The API handler becomes a thin delegation wrapper.

issues.summary: QueryResults is a namespace wrapper holding two unrelated DTOs; top-level classes would be simpler
evidence: QueryResults has only a private constructor and two static nested classes: WordSketchResult and ConcordanceResult
All callers already qualify with `QueryResults.WordSketchResult` / `QueryResults.ConcordanceResult`, gaining no brevity from the wrapper
The two DTOs have no shared base type, no shared logic, and different domains (sketch scoring vs concordance display)
issues.suggestion: Promote WordSketchResult and ConcordanceResult to top-level public classes in the `query` package. Remove the QueryResults wrapper.

issues.summary: SimpleTagger exposes both a public constructor and two static factory methods without clear guidance on which to use
evidence: Public constructor `new SimpleTagger()` on line 111
Static factory `SimpleTagger.create()` on line 215 delegating to the constructor
Static factory `SimpleTagger.create(String lexiconFile)` on line 222 — the only one adding value
The no-arg factory is a pure alias for the constructor and adds no information
issues.suggestion: Remove the no-arg `create()` factory. Either keep only the public constructor (and add a separate `loadLexicon` call) or make the constructor private and route all construction through the factories.

issues.summary: CQLParser.parse() and parseFull() return different types (CQLPattern vs ParsedCQL) with no ergonomic guide
evidence: parse() returns CQLPattern (high-level DTO), parseFull() returns ParsedCQL (internal intermediate type with alternatives and agreement rules)
ParsedCQL is a package-private inner class; external callers of parseFull() would receive it as a raw type without good IDE guidance
Both methods call the same parseInternal(); the split creates confusion about which API to prefer
issues.suggestion: Expose only one public return type. If external callers need alternatives/agreement rules, add them to CQLPattern. Make ParsedCQL package-private and unexpose parseFull() or rename it to make the richer return type obvious.

issues.summary: pom.xml declares `lucene.version=8.11.1` but it is never referenced in any dependency
evidence: pom.xml line 18: `<lucene.version>8.11.1</lucene.version>`
grep for `${lucene.version}` in pom.xml returns no matches — the property is orphaned
Lucene is transitively brought in via blacklab-engine; the standalone property creates a false impression that Lucene version is pinned here
issues.suggestion: Remove the unused `lucene.version` property. If Lucene version pinning is needed, add an explicit Lucene dependency with `<scope>provided</scope>` or use `<dependencyManagement>` to constrain it.

issues.summary: junit-platform-launcher version is hardcoded to 1.9.0 while all other JUnit deps use the `${junit.version}` property
evidence: pom.xml lines 66-72: junit-jupiter-api and junit-jupiter-engine use `${junit.version}` (5.9.0)
pom.xml lines 73-78: junit-platform-launcher uses hardcoded `1.9.0` instead of a property
Platform 1.9.0 corresponds to Jupiter 5.9.0 so they are compatible today, but future upgrades via `${junit.version}` would leave the launcher on 1.9.0
issues.suggestion: Add a `<junit.platform.version>1.9.0</junit.platform.version>` property (or derive it via `${junit.version}` with a suitable mapping) and reference it in the launcher dependency.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateFromPattern always returns "unknown" — method body is a dead stub
evidence: Lines 164-170: the method calls findLabelPosition() into `pos2Index`, then unconditionally `return "unknown";` regardless of pos2Index value
The method is called by BlackLabQueryExecutor but callers never receive a useful value from it
A sibling method extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition() already does the intended work
issues.suggestion: Remove extractCollocateFromPattern entirely and update any call sites to use extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition or extractCollocateFromMatch directly, which already implement the correct logic.

issues.summary: Several logger calls use string concatenation instead of SLF4J parameterized logging
evidence: Main.java line 66: `logger.error("Unknown command: " + command)`
SimpleTagger.java line 242: `logger.info("Loaded " + lexicon.size() + " entries from lexicon")`
All other logger calls in the codebase correctly use parameterized `{}` placeholders
issues.suggestion: Replace string concatenation: `logger.error("Unknown command: {}", command)` and `logger.info("Loaded {} entries from lexicon", lexicon.size())`.

issues.summary: Relation-type discriminators "SURFACE" and "DEP" are magic string literals scattered across 7 separate if-checks
evidence: WordSketchApiServer: "SURFACE" appears at lines 116, 267, 310; "DEP" appears at lines 140, 356, 411
GrammarConfigLoader line 538: `!"DEP".equals(relationType)`
No constant or enum is defined for these type discriminators
issues.suggestion: Define a `RelationType` enum (or String constants) with SURFACE and DEP values in GrammarConfigLoader or a shared model class. Replace all string literal comparisons with the enum.

issues.summary: convertConlluToWplChunks uses multi-statement single lines that reduce readability
evidence: Line 187: `w.close(); w = null; chunks++; sentencesInChunk = 0;` — four statements on one line
Line 201: `w.write(line); w.write('\n');` — two statements on one line
These are not hot-path optimizations; the style is inconsistent with the rest of the file
issues.suggestion: Split each statement to its own line for readability. The compiler produces identical bytecode.

issues.summary: Javadoc for handleVisualRadial contains the summary sentence twice
evidence:
Lines 871-873: `/** 
 * Render radial plot.
 * Render radial plot.
 * POST /api/visual/radial`
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicate sentence so the Javadoc reads: `/** Render radial plot. POST /api/visual/radial ...`
